body:
### Why this matters

Amazon SageMaker Notebook Instances running on Amazon Linux 2 platform identifiers (`notebook-al2-v1`, `notebook-al2-v2`, or `notebook-al2-v3`) stop receiving security updates after June 30, 2026. AWS also documents that AL2 notebook instances are vulnerable to `CVE-2026-24747` because the required PyTorch fix path depends on AL2023 rather than AL2.

### What this control checks

This control finds SageMaker Notebook Instances whose `platformIdentifier` is still set to an AL2 platform. These notebooks should be migrated to `notebook-al2023-v1` before the provider cutoff.

### Operational note

AWS documents this as an in-place platform migration that preserves data in `/home/ec2-user/SageMaker/`, but teams still need to validate lifecycle configuration scripts, package installation steps, kernels, and instance-type compatibility on AL2023.
name: Ensure SageMaker Notebook Instances use a supported platform identifier
description: SageMaker notebook instances running deprecated Amazon Linux 2 platforms
timedControlSummary: SageMaker Notebook Instances on Amazon Linux 2 should be migrated before July 1, 2026, when stopped AL2 notebooks can no longer be restarted.
timedControlDisruptionRisk: Late migration leaves notebooks without security updates and can block restarts or force rushed changes to lifecycle scripts, kernels, and instance types.
remediationDescription:
## AWS remediation

> **Service-wide migration (recommended):** migrate all remaining SageMaker Notebook Instances from AL2 to `notebook-al2023-v1` as one tracked platform upgrade. This is better than fixing notebooks one by one because AL2023 changes package management (`yum` to `dnf`), default platform behavior, supported instance types, and available ML images.

### Before you migrate

1. Inventory the affected notebook instances and note their `platformIdentifier`, `instanceType`, lifecycle configuration, and any custom bootstrap steps.
2. Review lifecycle configuration scripts and replace `yum` commands with `dnf` where needed.
3. Confirm the notebook uses an instance type supported on AL2023. AWS documents that `ml.p2`, `ml.p3`, `ml.p3dn`, `ml.inf1`, and `ml.g3` are not supported on AL2023 notebook instances.
4. Test the change on a non-production notebook first, especially if the notebook depends on custom kernels, native libraries, or older PyTorch builds.

### AWS Console

1. Open the SageMaker AI console and go to `Notebook instances`.
2. Select the affected notebook instance.
3. If the notebook is not already `Stopped`, choose `Stop` and wait for the status to become `Stopped`.
4. Choose `Edit`.
5. Change `Platform identifier` to `notebook-al2023-v1`.
6. If the current instance type is not supported on AL2023, choose a supported instance type before saving.
7. Choose `Update notebook instance`, then start the notebook and validate notebooks, kernels, lifecycle scripts, and package installs.

### AWS CLI

Inspect the current platform and instance type:

```bash
aws sagemaker describe-notebook-instance \
  --notebook-instance-name <notebook-name> \
  --query '{NotebookInstanceName:NotebookInstanceName,PlatformIdentifier:PlatformIdentifier,InstanceType:InstanceType,NotebookInstanceStatus:NotebookInstanceStatus}'
```

Stop the notebook if needed:

```bash
aws sagemaker stop-notebook-instance \
  --notebook-instance-name <notebook-name>
aws sagemaker wait notebook-instance-stopped \
  --notebook-instance-name <notebook-name>
```

Update the notebook to AL2023:

```bash
aws sagemaker update-notebook-instance \
  --notebook-instance-name <notebook-name> \
  --platform-identifier notebook-al2023-v1
```

If the current instance type is not supported on AL2023, change the instance type in the same update:

```bash
aws sagemaker update-notebook-instance \
  --notebook-instance-name <notebook-name> \
  --platform-identifier notebook-al2023-v1 \
  --instance-type <supported-instance-type>
```

Start the notebook again and wait until it is back in service:

```bash
aws sagemaker start-notebook-instance \
  --notebook-instance-name <notebook-name>
aws sagemaker wait notebook-instance-in-service \
  --notebook-instance-name <notebook-name>
```

Validate the new platform:

```bash
aws sagemaker describe-notebook-instance \
  --notebook-instance-name <notebook-name> \
  --query '{PlatformIdentifier:PlatformIdentifier,InstanceType:InstanceType,NotebookInstanceStatus:NotebookInstanceStatus}'
```

### Deadlines to plan around

- `June 30, 2026`: Amazon Linux 2 notebook instances reach end of support and stop receiving security updates or bug fixes.
- `July 1, 2026`: you can no longer create new or restart stopped AL2 notebook instances.
- `July 15, 2026`: SageMaker AI starts attempting to upgrade remaining AL2 notebook instances to AL2023.

### References

- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/nbi-al2.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/nbi-al2023.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/nbi-update.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/sagemaker/update-notebook-instance.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/sagemaker/wait/
- https://aws.amazon.com/amazon-linux-2/faqs/

timedControlRules.summary: SageMaker Notebook Instances on the deprecated notebook-al2-v1 platform should be migrated to AL2023 before restart restrictions apply.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: AL2 notebook instances stop receiving security updates and cannot be safely left for last-minute migration.
timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Set platformIdentifier to notebook-al2023-v1 and use a notebook instance type supported on AL2023.

timedControlRules.summary: SageMaker Notebook Instances on the deprecated notebook-al2-v2 platform should be migrated to AL2023 before restart restrictions apply.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: AL2 notebook instances stop receiving security updates and cannot be safely left for last-minute migration.
timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Set platformIdentifier to notebook-al2023-v1 and use a notebook instance type supported on AL2023.

timedControlRules.summary: SageMaker Notebook Instances on notebook-al2-v3 should be migrated to AL2023 before July 1, 2026.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: AL2 notebook instances stop receiving security updates and cannot be restarted after the provider cutoff if they are stopped.
timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Set platformIdentifier to notebook-al2023-v1 and use a notebook instance type supported on AL2023.
